nodes.label: AI as co-coder for knowledge augmentation and speed
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement that undermines learning
nodes.grounding_span: using the AI as a co-coder to help me augment my knowledge and speed up general development

nodes.label: Iterative collaborative development with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I like to go back and forth and iterate through the development alongside the model

nodes.label: AI as second set of eyes for code review
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or incomplete assistant
nodes.grounding_span: It can be extremely useful to have a second set of eyes on your work

nodes.label: AI reliability and completeness
nodes.grounding_span: it gave me something that looked good and somewhat fuctioned, but it was incomplete in main areas

nodes.label: AI as junior developer or coworker
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for human staff
nodes.grounding_span: I see it becoming more like my own Jr dev that can do more independently or like a coworker, rather than a replacement

nodes.label: AI impact on junior developer autonomy
nodes.label_negative: AI causing skill atrophy or replacement
nodes.grounding_span: It's allowing them to do more work without needing to stop their or the more Sr dev's workflow

nodes.label: Learning and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I'm actually learning at the same time rather than just blindly copying and pasting

nodes.label: Efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: significantly cut down on development time

nodes.label: Code quality and correctness
nodes.grounding_span: make sure I'm doing it to the best of my ability

nodes.label: Collaboration and team dynamics
nodes.grounding_span: we still hold regular code review meetings and one on one development sessions

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as collaborative learning partner
nodes.grounding_span: I like to go back and forth and iterate through the development alongside the model

nodes.label: Frustration with AI incomplete outputs
nodes.grounding_span: the fact that I basically put in 'to do later' placeholders was really frustrating

nodes.label: Optimistic vision of AI as coworker
nodes.grounding_span: I see it becoming more like my own Jr dev that can do more independently or like a coworker

nodes.label: Concern about AI replacing entry-level positions
nodes.grounding_span: There is a slight fear that eventually the models might get good enough to replace some of the staff

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through back-and-forth interaction
nodes.grounding_span: I like to go back and forth and iterate through the development alongside the model

nodes.label: Strategic reuse of existing code and selective AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I try to reuse my own code, especially if it's been commented and already optimized, whenever possible
